title: sulettaria surculosa
intro: Sulettaria surculosa, also known as the 'False Ashwagandha', is a plant species in the Acanthaceae family, native to tropical Africa, characterized by its small, woody stems and small white or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-stress effects. The health benefits of Sulettaria surculosa include reducing anxiety and stress levels, improving sleep quality, and enhancing overall well-being. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea, powder, or infusion, which can be used to promote relaxation and balance in the body.
